inline_review:
commit 7abaad53bf33f313cc64366596f6a842c3337ace
Author: Youngjun Park <youngjun.park@lge.com>

PM: hibernate: fix spurious GFP mask WARNING in uswsusp path

This commit introduces pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe() to prevent a warning when
saved_gfp_count is 0 during hibernation snapshot ioctls. The uswsusp path
now uses this safe wrapper to skip restoring the GFP mask when it hasn't
been restricted.

> diff --git a/include/linux/suspend.h b/include/linux/suspend.h
> index b02876f1ae38..7777931d88a5 100644
> --- a/include/linux/suspend.h
> +++ b/include/linux/suspend.h

[ ... ]

> diff --git a/kernel/power/main.c b/kernel/power/main.c
> index 5f8c9e12eaec..e610a8c8b7ff 100644
> --- a/kernel/power/main.c
> +++ b/kernel/power/main.c
> @@ -36,6 +36,13 @@
>  static unsigned int saved_gfp_count;
>  static gfp_t saved_gfp_mask;
>  
> +void pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe(void)
> +{
> +	if (!saved_gfp_count)
> +		return;

Is it safe to check saved_gfp_count before asserting the
system_transition_mutex is held?

Both pm_restore_gfp_mask() and pm_restrict_gfp_mask() assert
WARN_ON(!mutex_is_locked(&system_transition_mutex)) to ensure this shared
state is protected. Adding the assertion at the top of this wrapper would
ensure the locking invariant is always validated.

> +	pm_restore_gfp_mask();
> +}
> +
>  void pm_restore_gfp_mask(void)
>  {
>  	WARN_ON(!mutex_is_locked(&system_transition_mutex));
> diff --git a/kernel/power/user.c b/kernel/power/user.c
> index 3e41544b99d5..41cff6a89a1c 100644
> --- a/kernel/power/user.c
> +++ b/kernel/power/user.c
> @@ -306,7 +306,7 @@ static long snapshot_ioctl(struct file *filp, unsigned int cmd,
>  	case SNAPSHOT_UNFREEZE:
>  		if (!data->frozen || data->ready)
>  			break;
> -		pm_restore_gfp_mask();
> +		pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe();

If a user-space process successfully calls the SNAPSHOT_FREEZE ioctl,
data->frozen is set to true, but pm_restrict_gfp_mask() is not called.
If the process then exits or closes the device without further actions,
does snapshot_release() unconditionally call pm_restore_gfp_mask() and
trigger the same warning?

It looks like snapshot_release() might also need to be updated to use
pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe().

>  		free_basic_memory_bitmaps();
>  		data->free_bitmaps = false;
>  		thaw_processes();
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"The patch fails to replace `pm_restore_gfp_mask()` with `pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe()` in the device cleanup path (`snapshot_release()`).","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"In `kernel/power/user.c`, `snapshot_release()` is called when the `/dev/snapshot` file descriptor is closed. If a user-space process successfully calls the `SNAPSHOT_FREEZE` ioctl, `data->frozen` is set to `true`, but `pm_restrict_gfp_mask()` is NOT called, meaning `saved_gfp_count` remains 0. If the process then exits or closes the device without further actions, `snapshot_release()` sees `data->frozen == true` and unconditionally calls `pm_restore_gfp_mask()`. This triggers the exact same `WARN_ON(!saved_gfp_count)` that this patch attempts to fix in the ioctl paths. The call in `snapshot_release()` must also be updated to `pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe()` to fully fix the issue."},{"problem":"`pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe()` accesses shared state before asserting that the required lock is held.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"Both `pm_restrict_gfp_mask()` and `pm_restore_gfp_mask()` explicitly assert `WARN_ON(!mutex_is_locked(&system_transition_mutex))` at the beginning of the function because `saved_gfp_count` is protected by this mutex. By checking `if (!saved_gfp_count)` and returning before calling the inner function, `pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe()` skips the lock assertion when the count is 0. While current callers in `snapshot_ioctl()` and `snapshot_release()` hold the correct lock, this bypasses the locking invariant validation and could silently hide locking bugs if invoked without the mutex in the future. The `WARN_ON(!mutex_is_locked(&system_transition_mutex))` assertion should be placed at the top of the wrapper."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 7abaad53bf33f313cc64366596f6a842c3337ace
Author: Youngjun Park <youngjun.park@lge.com>

PM: hibernate: fix spurious GFP mask WARNING in uswsusp path

This commit introduces pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe() to prevent a warning when

> +		return;

Both pm_restore_gfp_mask() and pm_restrict_gfp_mask() assert

> +	pm_restore_gfp_mask();
> +}
> +

>  {
>  	WARN_ON(!mutex_is_locked(&system_transition_mutex));
> diff --git a/kernel/power/user.c b/kernel/power/user.c
> index 3e41544b99d5..41cff6a89a1c 100644
> --- a/kernel/power/user.c
> +++ b/kernel/power/user.c
> @@ -306,7 +306,7 @@ static long snapshot_ioctl(struct file *filp, unsigned int cmd,

>  		if (!data->frozen || data->ready)
>  			break;
> -		pm_restore_gfp_mask();
> +		pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe();

If a user-space process successfully calls the SNAPSHOT_FREEZE ioctl,
